card game where you can't see your own c.response:
I'd recommend these Android apps for card games where you can't see your own card:

1. **Headbands** - A classic guessing game where you wear a card on your forehead that only others can see. You ask yes/no questions to figure out what your card is.

2. **Post-it Note Game** - Similar mechanic where players stick notes on each other's foreheads without them knowing what's written. Popular party game format.

3. **Akinator** - While not exactly a card game, it's a mind-reading game where the app tries to guess what you're thinking of while you answer questions.

4. **Codenames** - A word-based team game where you give clues to help teammates guess words. The hidden information mechanic shares similar appeal.

5. **Hanabi** - A cooperative card game where players can see everyone else's cards but not their own, requiring communication and deduction.

The best match for the specific mechanic you're describing would be **Headbands** or **Hanabi**, as both directly feature the "can't see your own card" gameplay element.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
Here are the best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for playing **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**):

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Overview:** A dedicated, single-player adaptation of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **Features:** In this game, you play head-to-head against an AI dealer. Because you can only see the AI's card and not your own, you must read the AI's "tells" and reactions to decide whether to bet or fold. It features three difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard), each with distinct AI bluffing styles. 
* **Pros:** It is completely free, has no ads or in-app purchases, and works entirely offline.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused version of the classic forehead card game.
* **Features:** This app is designed to let you play the game digitally with friends and family. You predict your chances of winning by looking only at your opponent's card, trying to read their facial expressions, and using psychological tactics to bluff them out of the round. 

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A straightforward utility and simulation app for playing the game in a group setting.
* **Features:** It acts as a digital dealer for local multiplayer groups. Instead of needing a physical deck of cards to stick to your foreheads, the app distributes the digital cards so players can easily see everyone else's hand on screen except their own.

---

*Note on Terminology:* If you search the Play Store, you will also see many results for **Teen Patti** (often subtitled as "Indian Poker"). While Teen Patti is a highly popular 3-card game in India, it is a variant of traditional poker/brag rather than the "card-on-the-forehead" Blind Man's Bluff mechanic. For the true Blind Man's Bluff experience, **Blindex** or **Indian Poker - Card Game** are your best options.

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking to play **Forehead Poker** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Blind Man's Bluff**) on your Android device, several real, existing apps on the Google Play Store cater to this specific card game:

### 1. **Blindex**
* **Best for:** Single-player offline gameplay.
* **Description:** **Blindex** is a dedicated, 100% free, and fully offline single-player Blind Man's Bluff app. In this game, you play head-to-head against an AI dealer. You can see the AI's card on your screen, and the AI can see yours, but neither of you can see your own card. You must read the AI's animated facial expressions (tells) to guess your card and place your bets. It features three difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard) with different bluffing styles, and contains no ads or in-app purchases.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Best for:** Local multiplayer with friends.
* **Description:** This app is designed to let you play the classic forehead poker game with friends and family. It provides a digital deck where players can hold their devices up or pass them around to predict victory based solely on looking at their opponents' cards. It's a great party tool to facilitate the psychological and reading aspects of the game without needing a physical deck of cards.

### 3. **Holodek**
* **Best for:** A polished, premium multiplayer experience.
* **Description:** **Holodek** is a curated multi-game app that features a collection of highly polished, ad-free games to play with friends. One of its core featured games is **Blind Man's Bluff** (Forehead Poker), designed for 2 to 4 players with quick 2-minute sessions. It is a great option if you want a clean, aesthetic interface to play with friends online or on a commute.

### 4. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Best for:** Simple, lightweight group play.
* **Description:** A straightforward simulation app specifically built for playing Indian Poker. It is designed to be used by multiple people in the same room, serving as a virtual card dealer that keeps your own card hidden from you while displaying it to the other players.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
Here are several real Android apps available on the Google Play Store that feature card games where you cannot see your own cards:

### 1. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)

You must study their expressions, analyze their betting patterns, and use psychology to guess whether your hidden card is higher or lower than theirs before placing your bet.

The core mechanic is that you hold your cards facing outward—you can see everyone else's cards, but you cannot see your own. You must rely on limited, strategic clues from your teammates to figure out what you are holding and play the cards in the correct order.

* **Description:** If you want to play this style of game solo, Blindex is a free, offline single-player version of Blind Man's Bluff. You have to read the virtual AI opponents' "tells" and betting habits across multiple difficulty levels to deduce the value of your own card.

### 4. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Description:** A lightweight, straightforward multiplayer simulator designed for playing the classic forehead-card game with friends. It provides a clean digital deck and setup to facilitate local multiplayer rounds where everyone's card is public to the group except to the person holding it.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The game you are thinking of is a popular party genre often referred to as "headbands" or "charades." In these games, you select a category, place your phone on your forehead facing your friends, and try to guess the word or card on the screen using their clues. 

The best and most popular Android apps for this on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. 
* **How it works:** You choose from dozens of themed decks (like Celebrities, Movies, Animals, or Accents), hold the phone to your forehead, and guess the words based on your friends' clues before the timer runs out. 
* **Controls:** Tilt your phone down when you guess correctly, or tilt it up to pass. It also has a feature that lets you record video of your friends acting out the clues so you can watch it later.

### 2. **Charades & Headbands Game** (by Maple Media)
Also known as *"Charades App - What am I?"*, this is one of the most popular free alternatives on Android.
* **How it works:** It features over 60 themed decks ranging from movies, TV shows, and video games to music and science. 
* **Controls:** It uses the same intuitive tilt-to-correct (down) and tilt-to-pass (up) mechanics. You can also easily create your own custom decks to play with friends.

### 3. **Guess Up - Word Party Charades** (by Cosmicode)
A highly polished and feature-rich charades game.
* **How it works:** It offers over 100 fun decks (including pop culture categories like Netflix, Harry Potter, and Superheroes). 
* **Key Features:** It includes a dedicated "Team Mode" for larger group competitions, allows you to record and save your gameplay videos, and supports playing in over 25 different languages.

### 4. **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**
A great, ad-free option designed specifically for family game nights and parties.
* **How it works:** It features 48 unique decks and over 6,500 challenges. It is highly praised for its clean, intuitive design and seamless multiplayer experience.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are the best single-player, offline bluffing card games (and closely related bluffing games) available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **Description:** This is a dedicated digital version of the classic card game "Cheat" (also known as BS, Bluff, or *I Doubt It*). Designed specifically for offline single-player gameplay, you face off against three smart CPU opponents. You play cards face-down and declare their rank, deciding whether to tell the truth or bluff to empty your hand first. The game features a title system (Liar, Honest, Hunter) and strategic items like Lie Counters and Bluff Shields to spice up the gameplay.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game**
* **Description:** Another excellent offline adaptation of the classic "I Doubt It" card game. It offers a 2-player mode for quick 1v1 matches against a smart AI, as well as a 4-player mode with multiple CPU opponents. It requires absolutely no internet connection and features smooth card animations, dynamic difficulty, and daily quests to keep things engaging.

### 3. **Love Letter - Strategy Card Game**
* **Description:** This is the official digital adaptation of the award-winning physical card game. While it supports online play, it features a robust offline single-player mode where you play against computer AI. It is a fast-paced game of risk, deduction, and bluffing. Players must strategically play and hold cards to deliver their love letter to the princess while discrediting and eliminating rival suitors.

### 4. **Liar's Dice Online Multiplayer** (by YOAMB B.V.)
* **Description:** Although "Online Multiplayer" is in the title, this app features a fully functional "Offline AI practice" mode that does not require an internet connection. It is a digital version of the classic bluffing game (also known as Perudo or Pirate Dice). While it uses dice instead of cards, the core gameplay is pure bluffing: players roll hidden dice and take turns bidding on the total number of dice on the table, relying heavily on probability, bluffing, and calling out the AI's bluffs.

### 5. **Poker Offline: Texas Holdem**
* **Description:** If you want the ultimate card game of bluffing, this app lets you play classic Texas Hold'em completely offline. You play against offline AI opponents whose skill levels scale with the stakes. It features various tournaments, a guest mode (no registration required), and is perfect for practicing your poker face and bluffing strategies without risking real money.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Poker Deckbuilder
* **Description:** *Balatro* is a massive hit that blends classic poker hands with game-changing roguelike mechanics. You play illegal poker hands to score points, but the real strategy lies in collecting and combining over 150 unique "Joker" cards that modify the rules, trigger massive multipliers, and create wild combos. It is a premium, fully offline game with endless replayability.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Widely considered the gold standard of the deckbuilding genre, *Slay the Spire* tasks you with climbing a procedurally generated tower. You choose from four distinct characters, each with their own unique card pool and playstyle. As you defeat enemies, you draft new cards and collect powerful relics to build a synergistic deck. It is a premium game that runs flawlessly offline.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy RPG Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Built from the ground up for mobile devices, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode and features a rich dark fantasy aesthetic. It combines classic deckbuilding with RPG elements, allowing you to choose a class (like Warrior, Rogue, or Seeker) and wield class-specific weapons and magic. It is a premium game with over 1,100 cards, zero ads, and no microtransactions.

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Fairytale Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this beautifully illustrated game has you fighting through a dark forest filled with monsters. It plays in portrait mode and offers a variety of classes (like Knight, Ranger, and Alchemist), each requiring a completely different strategy. The base game is free to play offline, with optional one-time purchases to unlock additional classes and story chapters.

### 5. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Description:** If you want something different from traditional deckbuilders, *Card Thief* is a brilliant tactical puzzle game. Played on a 3x3 grid of cards, you guide a thief card through a deck of guards, torches, and obstacles to steal treasure and escape undetected. It is highly strategic, quick to play, and works entirely offline.

### 6. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **Genre:** Pirate-Themed Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Similar to *Slay the Spire* but set on the high seas, *Pirates Outlaws* has you navigate different maps, manage your ship's ammo, and battle outlaw crews. It features over 15 playable characters (each with pre-made decks and unique abilities) and hundreds of cards to collect. It is a premium game that is highly addictive and perfect for offline play.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) is called **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline, single-player card game where you play head-to-head against an AI dealer [1]. Key features include:
*   **Game Mechanic:** You hold a card face-out against your forehead so you cannot see it, while the AI opponent can [1].
*   **AI Tells:** The AI shows flinches, smirks, or stares that give you information about your card [1].
*   **Difficulty Levels:** Choose from three difficulty levels, each with a different AI bluffing style and tell to read [1].
*   **Offline & Free:** It runs fully offline with no internet required, is 100% free, and has no ads or in-app purchases [1].
*   **Availability:** It is available worldwide on the Google Play Store for Android [1].

Another app named **Bluff Online** exists, but it is a different card game where the goal is to get rid of your cards by playing them face-down and lying about their value, not the Blind Man's Bluff poker variant [2].

**Recommended App Name:** **Blindex** (available on Google Play for Android).

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you’re describing—where you can’t see your own card—is most likely **Old Maid** (also known as **Old Bachelor**), and a clean, minimal Android app that supports this game is **52**.

- **52** is a free, no-login card game app available on Android that lets you play classic games like Old Maid with friends via a shared link [4].  
- In **Old Maid**, players look at their own cards but keep them hidden from others, and the goal is to avoid holding the joker (the “Old Maid”) at the end [5].

If you prefer a dedicated app for Old Maid specifically, you can also try searching **“Old Maid card game”** on Google Play, as several apps exist with that exact title. However, **52** is the most streamlined option for playing traditional card games like Old Maid without setup [4].

For other popular deck-building card game apps on Android (if you’re open to alternatives), consider:
- **Star Realms** or **Hero Realms** (deck-building roguelike card games) [7]  
- **Card Guardians** (roguelike RPG card war game) [3]

But for the classic “can’t see your own card” experience, **52** with **Old Maid** is your best match.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android card game where you hold the phone to your forehead is called **Heads Up!** (developed by Warner Bros.), though a similar app named **Charades & Headbands Game** also uses this exact mechanic.

| Feature | **Heads Up!** | **Charades & Headbands Game** |
|---|---|---|
| **App ID** | `com.wb.headsup` [3] | `net.artgamestudio.charadesapp` [1] |
| **Core Mechanic** | One player holds the phone to their forehead like a headband; friends shout clues to guess the word [3] | Place device on forehead and ask friends to help guess what is on the screen [1] |
| **How to Guess** | Tilt phone **down** to score a point; tilt **up** to skip [3] | Tilt phone **up** (correct) or **down** (pass) to continue [1] |
| **Clue Types** | Dancing, singing, mimes, accents, celebrities [3] | Dancing, singing, mimes, or verbal clues [1] |
| **Decks Available** | 60+ categories (animals, celebrities, accents) [3] | Over 60 charades decks, customizable [1] |

Both apps are free on Google Play and designed for social party play where the screen faces your friends while you hold it to your forehead [3][1]. If you specifically want the official Warner Bros. version, download **Heads Up!** [3]; for a similar charades-focused alternative, try **Charades & Headbands Game** [1].
